# Review CHANGES REQUESTED for PAN-913

# Review Synthesis — PAN-913 — 2026-05-12T19:25:00Z

## Verdict: CHANGES REQUESTED

## Context
- Manifest: `/home/eltmon/Projects/panopticon-cli/workspaces/feature-pan-913/.pan/review/agent-pan-913-review-ab98c4b4/context.json`
- Branch: `feature/pan-913`
- Workspace: `/home/eltmon/Projects/panopticon-cli/workspaces/feature-pan-913`
- PR: #916 — PAN-913

## Convoy Status
| Sub-role | Status | Blocking findings |
| --- | --- | --- |
| security | done | 0 |
| correctness | done | 1 |
| performance | blocked | 1 |
| requirements | done | 3 |

## Blocking Findings

### [correctness] Codex auth gate blocks non-OpenAI subscription agents — `src/dashboard/server/routes/agents.ts:2105`
The route-level spawn guard checks only `providerAuthMode === 'subscription'` before calling `checkCodexAuthStatus()` and returning 429 for expired/burned Codex auth. `getProviderAuthMode(spawnModel)` can return `subscription` for Anthropic subscription-auth models, so normal Claude subscription work agents can be blocked by stale Codex auth even though they do not use Codex or CLIProxy. The equivalent helper in `src/lib/agents.ts:177` correctly scopes the check to OpenAI providers; the dashboard route should apply the same provider constraint or delegate to that helper.

### [performance] Review context is incomplete because the manifest diff is truncated — `context.json`
The performance reviewer could not complete the review because `diff.truncated` is `true` and the raw diff contains only 9 of 25 changed file headers. The missing hunks include high-risk performance-sensitive files such as `src/dashboard/server/routes/codex-auth.ts`, `src/dashboard/frontend/src/lib/pending-codex-spawn.ts`, `src/dashboard/server/ws-terminal.ts`, `src/lib/agents.ts`, and `src/lib/work/done-preflight.ts`. The review role forbids independent broad diff discovery, so approval is blocked until the review context includes the changed hunks needed for performance review.

### [requirements] Re-auth re-bridge path still performs sync filesystem I/O — `src/lib/cliproxy.ts:244`
The completion route calls `bridgeCodexAuthToCliproxyAsync()` from `src/dashboard/server/routes/codex-auth.ts:159`, but that async bridge still calls sync `ensureDirs()` at `src/lib/cliproxy.ts:244`. `ensureDirs()` uses `mkdirSync` at `src/lib/cliproxy.ts:78-80`, so the server-reachable re-auth completion path can still block the dashboard server event loop. This violates both `reauth-completion.ac3` and the project policy against sync filesystem calls in dashboard server-reachable code.

### [requirements] Duplicate re-auth requests do not return the existing live session — `src/dashboard/server/routes/codex-auth.ts:109`
The vBRIEF requires duplicate re-auth requests to return the existing live session details. The endpoint detects an existing re-auth session, but returns HTTP 409 with `{ error: 'A Codex re-authentication session is already running.' }` at `src/dashboard/server/routes/codex-auth.ts:109-114`. This prevents a client from resuming/opening the existing flow through the API response and leaves `reauth-endpoint.ac3` unmet.

### [requirements] Re-auth terminal token is not one-time despite the prior review requirement — `src/dashboard/server/ws-terminal.ts:145`
The prior blocker required a server-issued one-time token for `/ws/terminal?session=<name>`. `validateReauthTerminalToken()` only compares the stored token at `src/dashboard/server/routes/codex-auth.ts:69-72`, and `ws-terminal.ts` validates it at `src/dashboard/server/ws-terminal.ts:145-163` without deleting or rotating it. The docs also claim tokens are invalidated on first attach at `skills/cliproxy/SKILL.md:141-143`, which the code does not implement.

## Non-blocking Findings

### [correctness] Broad 429 classifier queues non-Codex spawn failures — `src/dashboard/frontend/src/lib/pending-codex-spawn.ts:88`
`isCodexBlockedResponse()` treats any `429` with `blocked: true` as a Codex-auth-blocked spawn. The same `/api/agents` route also returns 429/`blocked: true` for other provider health and guardrail failures, so unrelated spawn failures can be saved and retried through the Codex re-auth path. This does not loop indefinitely because `retryPendingSpawn()` clears the pending spawn before retrying.

### [requirements] Settings page does not show the full required Codex status details — `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/Settings/SettingsPage.tsx:563`
The settings UI shows `OAuth`/`OAuth active`, but the requirement asks for `Subscription OAuth`, live status, email, and expiry time. The reviewer found email and status evidence but no `expiresAt` rendering.

### [requirements] Burned-token detection window does not match the stated one-hour requirement — `src/lib/codex-auth.ts:157`
The vBRIEF requires treating `refresh token has already been used` as burned when it appears within the last hour. The implementation scans a 500-line window but treats a burn line as stale after 5 minutes, so a 6-60 minute old burn can be ignored unless the requirement is updated.

## Clean Sub-roles
- security: no findings.

Source: /home/eltmon/Projects/panopticon-cli/workspaces/feature-pan-913/.pan/review/agent-pan-913-review-ab98c4b4/synthesis.md

## Required action

Fix every blocking review finding, commit the fixes, then re-request review with:

`pan review request PAN-913 -m "Fixed review issues"`

## REQUIRED: Fix the failing check, push, and request a new review

1. Read the error output above carefully
2. Fix the code causing the failure
3. Run the failing check locally to verify it passes
4. Commit every change
5. Invoke the /rebase-and-submit skill for PAN-913 — this is an atomic task. Because verification already ran once (a PR exists), the skill will push your branch and run `pan review request PAN-913 -m "Fixed test"` for you. NEVER curl `/api/review/...` or any dashboard endpoint — `pan review request` is the only supported re-entry point.

Do NOT stop between steps. Do NOT stop after pushing. Do NOT stop until `pan review request` has completed successfully.
